notes_md:
**Đen thui** means *pitch black* — extremely dark, with no light at all. It’s stronger and more dramatic than just *đen*.

It’s an adjective, used to describe places, objects, or even people/things that look completely black. You can put it after the noun (*trời đen thui*) or after linking verbs like *trở nên*, *nhìn*, *trông*.

It can also describe color, not just darkness — something very black (like clothes, skin after sun, burnt food).

Close synonym: **đen kịt** (also very dark, slightly more intense).
exampleSentencesTranslated: Last night the power went out so the whole room was pitch black.
I am scared to walk through the alley that is pitch black at night.
It rained heavily, and the road home looked pitch black and I couldn't see anything.
notes:
Đen thui means pitch black — extremely dark, with no light at all. It’s stronger and more dramatic than just đen.

It’s an adjective, used to describe places, objects, or even people/things that look completely black. You can put it after the noun (trời đen thui) or after linking verbs like trở nên, nhìn, trông.

It can also describe color, not just darkness — something very black (like clothes, skin after sun, burnt food).

Close synonym: đen kịt (also very dark, slightly more intense).
exampleSentences: Tối hôm qua mất điện nên cả phòng đen thui.
Em sợ đi qua con hẻm đen thui vào ban đêm.
Trời mưa to, đường về nhà nhìn đen thui không thấy gì.
